MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Apr-06-2005 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Vicky W. Chan for defendant and moving party
NO OTHER APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant's motion to quash or modify subpoena is granted in part:
The Court finds that plaintiff complied with the notice provisions of CCP Section 1985.3(b)(1) by service of the subpoenas duces tecum on the attorney of record for defendant Elizabeth Yip, an owner of both accounts that are the subject of the subpoenas.

The subpoenas are modified to only require production of records relating to Exhbits C and D to defendant's motion to quash. Plaintiff has not set forth sufficient facts to justify the complete production of the personal financial records of defendant Elizabeth Yip and non-parties Shee H. Yip and Eliza Chi Hui Howe.

The Court further orders that defendant execute a declaration verifying the facts set forth in the March 23, 2005 letter from her counsel relating to the accounts that held rent deposits, all disbursements related to the subject properties, and the holder of accounts / information regarding various accounts. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
